paper.title: Growth kinetics of spoilage bacteria during fermentation of cream cheese

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: The gamma concept expresses the effective growth rate as the product of the maximum growth rate and individual dimensionless gamma factors for each environmental variable, assuming multiplicative and independent effects.

parameters.name: maximum specific growth rate under optimal conditions

assumptions: Environmental factors have independent, multiplicative effects on growth rate.
Each gamma factor ranges from 0 (complete inhibition) to 1 (optimal conditions).

source_spans.text_context: The gamma concept expresses the effective growth rate as the product of the maximum growth rate and individual dimensionless gamma factors for each environmental variable, assuming multiplicative and independent effects.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Extended cardinal temperature model with minimum, optimum, and maximum temperatures, applicable across the full growth range including super-optimal temperatures.
mechanistic_equations.equation_latex: \gamma(T) = \frac{(T - T_{\max})(T - T_{\min})^2}{(T_{\mathrm{opt}} - T_{\min})\left((T_{\mathrm{opt}} - T_{\min})(T - T_{\mathrm{opt}}) - (T_{\mathrm{opt}} - T_{\max})(T_{\mathrm{opt}} + T_{\min} - 2T)\right)}
assumptions: Growth rate is zero at T_min and T_max, maximal at T_opt.

source_spans.text_context: Extended cardinal temperature model with minimum, optimum, and maximum temperatures, applicable across the full growth range including super-optimal temperatures.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Simple logistic growth equation for microbial populations, describing symmetric sigmoidal growth toward a carrying capacity.
assumptions: Symmetric growth curve around the inflection point.

source_spans.text_context: Simple logistic growth equation for microbial populations, describing symmetric sigmoidal growth toward a carrying capacity.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Dimensionless gamma factor representing the effect of pH on microbial growth rate, using minimum, optimum, and maximum pH values in a cardinal parameter model.
assumptions: Growth rate is zero at pH_min and pH_max.
Growth rate is maximal at pH_opt.
Assumes independent, multiplicative effect of pH on growth rate.

source_spans.text_context: Dimensionless gamma factor representing the effect of pH on microbial growth rate, using minimum, optimum, and maximum pH values in a cardinal parameter model.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Non-linear inactivation model using the Weibull distribution to describe microbial death curves that exhibit tailing or shoulder effects.

parameters.name: scale parameter (time for first log reduction)

assumptions: p = 1 reduces to the log-linear model.

source_spans.text_context: Non-linear inactivation model using the Weibull distribution to describe microbial death curves that exhibit tailing or shoulder effects.

mechanistic_equations.equation_description: Defines product shelf life as the time for the microbial population to reach a spoilage threshold concentration from a given initial contamination level.
assumptions: Exponential growth assumed throughout shelf life.
Spoilage occurs when a threshold concentration is reached.

source_spans.text_context: Defines product shelf life as the time for the microbial population to reach a spoilage threshold concentration from a given initial contamination level.
